prompt: Add a cost breakdown by region

app_definition.app_description: Analyze supply chain costs with key KPIs, regional breakdowns, category distribution, and detailed cost views.

components.title: Total Material Cost
components.sql_query: SELECT SUM(total_cost) AS total_material_cost FROM supply_chain;

components.title: Average Unit Cost
components.sql_query: SELECT AVG(unit_cost) AS avg_unit_cost FROM supply_chain;

components.title: Total Shipping Cost
components.sql_query: SELECT SUM(shipping_cost) AS total_shipping_cost FROM supply_chain;

components.title: Total Costs by Region
components.sql_query:
SELECT 
  region,
  SUM(total_cost) AS total_material_cost,
  SUM(shipping_cost) AS total_shipping_cost,
  SUM(warehouse_cost) AS total_warehouse_cost,
  SUM(total_cost + shipping_cost + warehouse_cost) AS total_all_in_cost
FROM supply_chain
GROUP BY region
ORDER BY total_all_in_cost DESC;

components.title: Cost Distribution by Category (All-in)
components.sql_query:
SELECT 
  category,
  SUM(total_cost + shipping_cost + warehouse_cost) AS total_all_in_cost
FROM supply_chain
GROUP BY category
ORDER BY total_all_in_cost DESC;

components.title: Detailed Cost Table
components.sql_query:
SELECT 
  order_id,
  order_date,
  supplier,
  region,
  product,
  category,
  quantity,
  unit_cost,
  total_cost,
  shipping_cost,
  warehouse_cost,
  (total_cost + shipping_cost + warehouse_cost) AS total_all_in_cost
FROM supply_chain
ORDER BY order_date ASC, order_id ASC;

components.title: Cost Breakdown by Region
components.sql_query:
SELECT 
  region,
  SUM(total_cost) AS total_material_cost,
  SUM(shipping_cost) AS total_shipping_cost,
  SUM(warehouse_cost) AS total_warehouse_cost,
  SUM(total_cost + shipping_cost + warehouse_cost) AS total_all_in_cost,
  AVG(unit_cost) AS avg_unit_cost,
  SUM(quantity) AS total_quantity
FROM supply_chain
GROUP BY region
ORDER BY total_all_in_cost DESC;

components.title: Total Material Cost
components.explanation: Total Material Cost: Displays a single metric value (1 data point)

components.title: Average Unit Cost
components.explanation: Average Unit Cost: Displays a single metric value (1 data point)

components.title: Total Shipping Cost
components.explanation: Total Shipping Cost: Displays a single metric value (1 data point)

components.title: Total Costs by Region
components.explanation: Total Costs by Region: Compares 5 categories

components.title: Cost Distribution by Category (All-in)
components.explanation: Cost Distribution by Category (All-in): Breaks down into 5 segments

components.title: Detailed Cost Table
components.explanation: Detailed Cost Table: Shows 500 rows across 12 columns

components.title: Cost Breakdown by Region
components.explanation: Cost Breakdown by Region: Shows 5 rows across 7 columns

overview.summary: This dashboard gives you a clear view of overall supply chain costs and then breaks them down by region so you can see where spend is concentrated and how cost structure differs geographically. You can compare total and average costs at a global level, then drill into regional totals, mix by category, and individual orders. The new regional breakdown table and bar chart together answer your request by showing total, shipping, warehouse, and all‑in costs, plus unit economics, for each region.

components.title: Total Material Cost
components.narration: This KPI shows your overall material spend across all regions, at about 16.78M in total. It gives you the global baseline against which each region’s material costs can be compared.

components.title: Average Unit Cost
components.narration: This card shows that, on average, you pay about 128.02 per unit across all orders and regions. You can use this as a benchmark to see which regions are buying above or below the global average in the regional breakdown.

components.title: Total Shipping Cost
components.narration: Here you see total shipping spend of roughly 520.13K across the entire supply chain. This helps you understand how much of your overall cost base is driven by logistics before looking at how that shipping cost is distributed by region.

components.title: Total Costs by Region
components.narration: This chart compares total costs across regions, showing that Africa has the highest all‑in cost at about 3.65M, followed closely by North America, Asia Pacific, and Latin America (each around 3.5–3.6M), with Europe slightly lower at about 3.13M. It also breaks out material, shipping, and warehouse components so you can see how each cost type contributes to the regional totals.

components.title: Cost Distribution by Category (All-in)
components.narration: This pie shows how your all‑in costs are split across categories, with Assembly (≈4.17M) and Electronics (≈4.10M) being the largest, followed by Mechanical, Hardware, and Materials. While not regional, it helps you understand which cost categories dominate your spend before layering on the regional view.

components.title: Detailed Cost Table
components.narration: This table lists individual orders with their region, product, category, quantities, and full cost breakdown, such as an Asia Pacific order for Rubber Seals totaling about 25.6K all‑in and a European order for Hydraulic Cylinders at about 66.0K. You can use it to trace regional cost patterns down to specific suppliers and products.

components.title: Cost Breakdown by Region
components.narration: This table directly delivers the regional cost breakdown you requested, showing for each region the totals for material, shipping, and warehouse costs, plus all‑in cost, average unit cost, and total quantity. For example, Africa has about 3.51M in material cost and the highest average unit cost at 135.26, while Latin America buys the most units (27,267) with a lower average unit cost of about 123.96, letting you compare both total spend and unit economics by region.
